evals.prompt: Initialize a new B2B internal tool with Vite, TanStack Router, docs/spec.md, harness workflow files, and concise CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for both Claude and Codex.
evals.expected_output: A new-project plan or implementation path that selects the Vite/TanStack stack, generates repo-local harness contracts, and keeps CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md concise with routed reference docs.
expectations: Chooses a plausible core plan and explains the stack choice.
Includes docs/spec.md, tasks/todos.md, tasks/lessons.md, docs/researches/, tasks/contracts/, tasks/reviews/, and .ai/harness/ in the generated workflow.
Treats docs/PROGRESS.md as milestone-only instead of the active execution log.

evals.prompt: This repo already exists. Fix AGENTS.md so Codex is required to sync tasks/todos.md and tasks/lessons.md, and make the final response mention which task files were updated.
evals.expected_output: An audit or implementation plan that strengthens AGENTS.md, aligns CLAUDE.md if needed, and updates any supporting protocol docs or tests to enforce the tasks-first contract.

files_not_contain.pattern: hooks as the only source of enforcement

expectations: Calls out repo-local task sync as the primary enforcement mechanism.
Treats plans/ as the plan catalog and the host-neutral active marker as the active selector instead of relying on docs/plan.md.
Updates the final response contract to mention changed tasks files.
Avoids treating hooks as the only source of enforcement.

evals.prompt: Migrate this older Claude Code repo to the current 3.0 harness contract. It still uses docs/TODO.md and writes active session notes into docs/PROGRESS.md.
evals.expected_output: A migration plan or implementation path that removes docs/TODO.md, installs the harness workflow files and helper scripts, and moves PROGRESS.md to milestone-only usage.
expectations: Replaces docs/TODO.md with tasks/todos.md as the primary task contract.
Installs docs/spec.md, tasks/reviews/, and .ai/harness/ as first-class harness artifacts.
Removes docs/plan.md compatibility pointers in favor of plans/ as the active-plan source of truth.
Adds repo-local task-sync enforcement such as scripts/check-task-sync.sh.
Updates migration guidance and scripts rather than only editing prose.
Installs a shared .ai/ hook layer and routes Claude hook settings through it.

evals.prompt: Audit this AI-assisted coding setup. Tell me whether Claude and Codex can both follow the same repo contract, and identify any workflow drift or missing files.
evals.expected_output: A concrete audit that inspects AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, tasks/, hooks, and reference docs, then identifies drift and recommends the minimal safe set of fixes.
expectations: Checks both Claude-specific hooks and cross-agent repo-local contracts.
Flags docs/PROGRESS.md misuse if it is acting as an execution log.
Flags duplicated active-plan state outside plans/ as workflow drift.
Flags missing docs/spec.md, tasks/reviews/, or .ai/harness/ as incomplete 3.0 migration.
Mentions migration or template updates when current files are out of sync with the skill.
Calls out when a repo only has .claude hook wiring but lacks the shared .ai/ automation layer.

evals.prompt: This repo has Skill Factory enabled with Codex as the primary agent. The state file shows 2 bug-fix sessions and 2 testing-conventions lessons — both one session away from the proposal threshold. Run the skill-factory-check script, simulate a third session that pushes both patterns over threshold, verify that pending proposals are created for both a workflow skill and a knowledge skill, and confirm that normal post-edit activity is not treated as optimization feedback unless explicit feedback is recorded.
evals.expected_output: A Codex-driven session that updates tasks/lessons.md with a third testing-conventions entry, runs skill-factory-check.sh to confirm state, executes the session-end hook to trigger pattern detection, verifies both a workflow proposal (bug-fix) and a knowledge proposal (testing-conventions) appear in ~/.claude/.skill-proposals.json, and keeps optimization hints tied to explicit feedback recorded through skill-factory-check.sh.
expectations: Reads .claude/.skill-factory-state.json to understand current pattern counts.
Uses AGENTS.md Skill Factory protocol to group lessons by theme.
Runs bash scripts/skill-factory-check.sh to inspect current state.
After the third session, both workflow and knowledge proposals reach pending status.
Adds evidence-aware proposal context including evidence score or correction count when reporting workflow proposal state.
Does not auto-create skills — proposals require explicit human promotion via skill-factory-create.sh.
Does not treat ordinary post-edit activity as optimization feedback.
Uses bash scripts/skill-factory-check.sh --record-feedback <slug> --signal <label> when explicit feedback is needed.

evals.prompt: Initialize a new AI quantitative trading project (Plan G) with factor research scaffolding, factor-lab scripts, and Plan G workflow guidance in the generated automation docs.
evals.expected_output: A Plan G initialization path that exposes factor research scaffolding, includes tasks/factors/registry.json plus factor-lab commands, and keeps the rest of the tasks-first workflow intact.
expectations: Selects Plan G or explicitly identifies the AI quantitative trading preset.
Mentions the factor registry as the source of truth for factor lifecycle state.
Includes the factor-lab new/promote/reject/check commands in the generated workflow discussion.
Preserves the standard tasks-first workflow alongside factor research scaffolding.

evals.prompt: Initialize this existing repo with the repo-harness workflow. Do not create a new Vite or app scaffold; just add the repo-local harness, hooks, docs/spec.md, tasks, plans, .ai/context, and .ai/harness.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-init, inspects the existing repo first, uses migrate-project-template --apply for the safe init path, and explicitly avoids app scaffolding.
expectations: Treats init as existing-repo adoption, not product scaffold creation.
Names repo-harness-init as the public command.
Keeps hook and docs initialization behind the migration engine.

evals.prompt: Scaffold a new Vite + TanStack Router internal tool with the repo-harness workflow attached from the beginning.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-scaffold, selects the Vite/TanStack plan catalog entry, creates the new project scaffold, and attaches the tasks-first harness.
expectations: Treats scaffold as new project or module creation.
Uses plan catalog A-K instead of ad hoc stack guessing.
Attaches the repo-local workflow after product scaffold creation.

evals.prompt: This older Claude repo has docs/TODO.md, docs/PROGRESS.md, and old .claude hook shims. Migrate it to the current repo-harness harness without deleting my notes.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-migrate, archives or preserves legacy docs, removes only known_generated stale shims, and verifies the tasks-first harness.
expectations: Preserves or archives user-authored legacy workflow docs.
Does not conflate migration with scaffold.
Uses the manifest-owned removal boundary.

evals.prompt: This repo already has the tasks-first harness. Upgrade it to the latest repo-harness contract and refresh managed helper files, but preserve _ref, _ops, custom hooks, and local env.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-upgrade, reads the inspector upgrade_plan, applies manifest-owned upgrade actions only, and preserves user-local/private surfaces.
expectations: Uses upgrade for current harness refresh, not migration.
Reads manifest-owned upgrade actions.
Preserves private and user-local paths.

evals.prompt: The agentic workflow is already installed, but task sync and hook routing are broken. Repair the current harness without migrating legacy docs or scaffolding a new app.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-repair, reproduces the failing path, fixes task sync or hook routing minimally, and reruns the relevant workflow gate.
expectations: Uses repair only after recognizing a current harness surface.
Traces the failing workflow path before fixing.
Keeps the fix targeted to task sync, hooks, handoff, context, policy, or helpers.

evals.prompt: Check whether this repo-harness harness is ready to merge. Run the workflow gates, inspector, task sync, and migration dry-run and give me the release readiness verdict.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-check, runs or names the required workflow gates, reports pass/fail evidence, and does not mutate repo files by default.
expectations: Uses check as a verification entrypoint, not a mutating repair.
Includes inspector and migration dry-run in the evidence set.
Gives a readiness verdict grounded in command output.

evals.prompt: Plan how we should change the repo-harness workflow command surface, but do not edit files yet.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-plan, inspects repo state, produces a decision-complete plan, and names the next action command without mutating files.
expectations: Keeps planning non-mutating by default.
Produces one recommended action path.
Separates plan output from implementation.

evals.prompt: Review this existing repo-harness plan for product, engineering, design, and DevEx issues before implementation.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-review, reads the plan, selects review dimensions, reports blocking issues first, and does not implement by default.
expectations: Treats review as plan-stage critique.
Covers product, engineering, design, and DevEx when relevant.
Keeps implementation separate from review.

evals.prompt: Autoplan the repo-harness command change: read the repo, make the planning decisions for me, run the workflow automatically, and ship through the default PR closeout.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-autoplan, runs plan then two self-review passes, executes the approved plan, runs check gates, and delegates PR closeout to repo-harness-ship.
expectations: Runs the full planning, self-review, execution, check, and ship sequence automatically.
Surfaces only material final decision gates.
Does not redirect into long interactive review unless automatic workflow execution is unsafe.

evals.prompt: This repo already has repo-harness. Add only a docs/payments capability boundary with local AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files; do not run full init.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-capability, uses capability-config add for explicit prefixes, validates with capability-resolver, and avoids full init, migrate, or upgrade.
expectations: Uses explicit prefixes instead of inferring broad directory globs.
Creates only requested local AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md contract files.
Does not run the full repo-harness-init path.

evals.prompt: Resolve the pending architecture drift request and update the architecture diagram if it helps, but do not refresh the whole harness.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-architecture, inspects docs/architecture/index.md and pending requests, uses capability matching when relevant, archives handled requests, and only generates a diagram through diagram-design when useful.
expectations: Treats hooks as architecture request recorders, not prose rewriters.
Keeps docs/architecture/requests pending-only after handling a request.
Does not run a full harness refresh.

evals.prompt: Prepare a Codex handoff for this long task and print the resume prompt. Do not run the full workflow check.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-handoff, runs prepare-codex-handoff or codex-handoff-resume as appropriate, verifies current.md and resume.md, and reports the exact next step without running /check by default.
expectations: Limits mutation to the handoff packet files.
Reports the resume prompt when requested.
Does not substitute handoff preparation for readiness verification.

evals.prompt: Check whether our deploy folder and private _ops setup are ready. Do not deploy anything.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request to repo-harness-deploy, reads the operations policy, checks deploy/ and _ops/ boundaries, runs check-deploy-sql-order, and reports readiness gaps without publishing or deploying.
expectations: Keeps the check read-only by default.
Separates tracked deploy assets from ignored private operations state.
Does not print secrets, tokens, or real environment values.

evals.prompt: Codex is not detecting this repo's hooks after migration. Audit and repair the hook workflow, and do not make hooks run autoresearch mutations in the background.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request as a runtime-harness hook workflow repair, checks the user-level Codex hook adapter, Codex Settings trust, repo-harness-hook resolution, the active target hook script, trace or events evidence, and keeps autoresearch as an agent-run loop rather than a background hook mutation.
expectations: Treats the task as runtime-harness hook workflow repair.
Checks Codex adapter config separately from Codex Settings trust.
Debugs through repo-harness-hook resolution, the active hook script, and hook-specific trace or events evidence.
Keeps any autoresearch hook advisory and self-host/dev-only rather than making it a default user-installed hook.
Keeps hooks as advisory guards over repo-local plans, tasks, contracts, checks, and handoff artifacts.
Does not silently run a full autoresearch mutation loop from PostEdit or Prompt hooks.

evals.prompt: The repo has no active plan but still has old plan files. Refresh the Codex handoff and make sure the resume packet does not point a fresh session at old work.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-handoff, runs prepare-codex-handoff or codex-handoff-resume, and reports that plan, contract, and notes are none when there is no active marker.
expectations: Does not infer active work from the newest plan file.
Keeps current user input above handoff recovery artifacts.
Reports the exact next step from current.md.

evals.prompt: Decide whether this request should use repo-harness-init or repo-harness-scaffold: I have an existing repo and only want hooks, docs/spec.md, tasks, plans, .ai/context, and .ai/harness added.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-init, names scaffold as the new-project path only, and avoids creating a Vite or other app scaffold.
expectations: Keeps existing-repo adoption separate from app scaffolding.
Names scaffold only as the out-of-scope route.
Uses inspector or migration engine before applying changes.

evals.prompt: Check release readiness. The workflow gates pass, but Waza has Codex staging drift and gbrain reports a warning. Give the verdict without silently treating those as pass or hard fail.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-check, keeps required workflow gates as hard pass/fail, reports Waza drift and gbrain warning as yellow flags, and treats missing CodeGraph or Codex health/check/mermaid as hard failures.
expectations: Separates hard workflow gates from advisory readiness.
Does not fail only because Waza staging drift or gbrain warning is present.
Does not claim clean readiness without listing yellow flags.

evals.prompt: This repo already has the harness installed. Use repo-harness-sprint to plan a program-level sprint for a notification feature: discuss the PRD, decompose it into an ordered backlog under plans/sprints/ linking the upper-layer PRD in plans/prds/ when present, and explain how each backlog task will execute and be tracked.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-sprint: PM+architect PRD discussion, sprint-backlog.sh init, an ordered backlog table with concrete acceptance per row, user approval before Approved status, then per-task execution through capture-plan (--source repo-harness-sprint) into the plan -> contract -> worktree flow with warn-only finish back-fill; tasks/todos.md stays the deferred-goal ledger.
expectations: Routes the request to repo-harness-sprint instead of plain repo-harness-plan.
Keeps tasks/todos.md as the deferred-goal ledger and places the backlog in plans/sprints/.
Requires a concrete acceptance line per backlog row before Approved status.
Executes each backlog task through the existing plan -> contract -> worktree gates.

evals.prompt: Run the route-nl-vs-ts shadow eval for the loop engine. Locate the mounted repo-harness skill root: use .skill-src when it exists, otherwise use .claude/skills/repo-harness. Create .ai/harness/runs/. Write scenarios with `bun "$SKILL_ROOT/scripts/route-nl-vs-ts-eval.ts" --emit-scenarios > .ai/harness/runs/route-nl-vs-ts-scenarios.json`. Read `$SKILL_ROOT/docs/reference-configs/loop-engine-nl-decision-table.md`, then make your own NL decision-table routing choices for every scenario and write `.ai/harness/runs/route-nl-vs-ts-decisions.json` with a top-level `decisions` array of `{scenario_id,intent,action,rationale}` objects. The intent and action fields MUST be exact strings from the scenario pack `allowed_intents` and `allowed_actions` arrays; do not invent synonyms such as enter_done_gate, capture_pending_plan, or scaffold_contract. Do not use `--write-expected-decisions` for the NL arm. Run `bun "$SKILL_ROOT/scripts/route-nl-vs-ts-eval.ts" --agent benchmark --decisions .ai/harness/runs/route-nl-vs-ts-decisions.json --out .ai/harness/runs/route-nl-vs-ts-report.json`. Final response must summarize compliance_rate, false_positive_count, false_negative_count, estimated_token_delta_per_prompt, and go/no-go. Do not edit source files.
evals.expected_output: A shadow-routing eval run that leaves a route-nl-vs-ts report under .ai/harness/runs/, compares the current TS verdict arm against NL decision-table self-routing, and reports compliance, false positives, false negatives, token delta, and go/no-go without changing runtime prompt-guard behavior.

commands_succeed: skill_root=.skill-src; if [ ! -e "$skill_root/scripts/route-nl-vs-ts-eval.ts" ]; then skill_root=.claude/skills/repo-harness; fi; bun "$skill_root/scripts/route-nl-vs-ts-eval.ts" --check-report .ai/harness/runs/route-nl-vs-ts-report.json

expectations: Runs the existing TS prompt-guard verdict as the A arm without changing it.
Uses the natural-language decision table as the B arm and writes agent-made route decisions.
Covers historical route regressions from lessons.md and hook runtime tests.
Reports compliance rate, false positives, false negatives, token delta, and go/no-go in .ai/harness/runs/.
Keeps classifier cutover out of scope.

evals.prompt: Use repo-harness-prd to generate a PRD from this product idea: notification preferences for a small SaaS app. Keep it compact, save it under plans/prds/, and summarize only the AI Quick-Read Card in the final response.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-prd, activates $geju for a direction pass, uses Claude-first claude -p --model opus drafting with Codex only as fallback, and writes a compact PRD under plans/prds/ with an AI Quick-Read Card, evidence-marked unknowns, acceptance scenarios, module behavior, data model, performance targets, and a developer handoff. The final response gives the Quick-Read Card, geju thesis, drafting path, and file path only.
expectations: Routes the request to repo-harness-prd instead of repo-harness-sprint.
Runs a geju direction pass before PRD drafting.
Uses Claude-first claude -p --model opus drafting and keeps Codex as fallback only.
Writes the PRD under plans/prds/ and does not create a sprint backlog.
Keeps competitor or SaaS vendor claims out unless explicitly verified.
Includes Quick-Read Card, acceptance scenarios, and developer handoff.

evals.prompt: Use repo-harness-sprint from-prd plans/prds/20260101-0000-sample-feature.prd.md to create a sprint backlog. Preserve the PRD as Source PRD, put the backlog under plans/sprints/, and do not write tasks/todos.md.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-sprint from-prd, reads the sample PRD, sets Source PRD, writes or describes an ordered sprint backlog under plans/sprints/ with machine-checkable acceptance lines traceable to the PRD, and keeps tasks/todos.md as the deferred-goal ledger.
expectations: Routes the request to repo-harness-sprint from-prd.
Keeps the PRD in plans/prds/ and places the backlog in plans/sprints/.
Backlog acceptance lines are machine-checkable and traceable to PRD scenarios.
Does not use tasks/todos.md as an execution backlog.

evals.prompt: Use repo-harness:goal to prepare a Codex or Claude /goal session for plans/prds/20260101-0000-sample-feature.prd.md. Require the detailed PRD or Sprint document as source of truth, produce the bounded /goal prompt, and do not start implementation if the source document is missing.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-goal / repo-harness:goal, requires a detailed PRD or Sprint artifact before native /goal continuation, prepares a bounded Codex/Claude /goal prompt with source of truth, scope, non-goals, acceptance checks, stop condition, and repo-harness workflow gates, and stops to request the PRD/Sprint document when missing.
expectations: Routes the request to repo-harness-goal instead of repo-harness-autoplan.
Requires a detailed PRD or Sprint artifact as the goal source of truth.
Produces a host-native /goal prompt with bounded scope, non-goals, acceptance checks, and stop condition.
Does not start implementation when the source PRD/Sprint document is missing.

evals.prompt: Use repo-harness:gptpro_setup to guide setup for both gptpro_browser and gptpro_mcp. The user has ChatGPT Pro in the browser and wants local repo-harness to use browser/session consults while ChatGPT can also connect back to the local repo through MCP.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-gptpro-setup / repo-harness:gptpro_setup, separates gptpro_browser local ChatGPT Web browser/session consults from gptpro_mcp ChatGPT Connector -> local MCP sidecar setup, runs or names browser-setup/browser-doctor and mcp setup/doctor, requires HTTPS tunnel/manual ChatGPT Connector steps, redacts local auth files, and states ChatGPT Pro is not OpenAI API quota.
expectations: Routes the request to repo-harness-gptpro-setup.
Keeps gptpro_browser and gptpro_mcp as separate setup lanes with different directions.
Requires browser doctor and MCP doctor verification before claiming success.
Keeps ChatGPT Pro Web access separate from OpenAI API quota or API keys.
Redacts local auth files and requires explicit HTTPS tunnel/manual Connector setup.

evals.prompt: Use repo-harness:gptpro to ask GPT Pro for architecture feedback on the current plan. If there is an existing GPT Pro session, continue it; otherwise start a new browser-session consult and write the result into the repo handoff area.
evals.expected_output: Routes to repo-harness-gptpro / repo-harness:gptpro, speaks in gptpro consult/read/continue/open language, maps the actual work to browser-doctor plus browser-consult or browser-followup, can read/open existing saved sessions with browser-session/browser-open, writes or names .ai/harness/handoff/gptpro-consult.md, and routes setup or gptpro_mcp requests back to repo-harness:gptpro_setup.
expectations: Routes the request to repo-harness-gptpro.
Uses GPT Pro wording for the user while keeping browser-* as the implementation engine.
Supports both new consults and continuing existing saved GPT Pro sessions.
Writes or names a repo-local handoff artifact for the GPT Pro result.
Routes setup and gptpro_mcp work back to repo-harness-gptpro-setup.
